seo.title: Building Agentic RAG with AG2 + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build agentic rag with AG2 + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Agentic RAG with AG2 enables multi-agent systems to autonomously plan retrieval queries, fetch from diverse sources, and synthesize attributed responses, powering advanced knowledge-intensive applications. Guardrails are essential due to high-risk failure modes like hallucination, off-topic spirals, and inconsistent cross-source synthesis in group chats. Vex integration provides runtime verification for source faithfulness and query accuracy, ensuring reliable outputs.

architectureOverview.heading: Agentic RAG Architecture with AG2
architectureOverview.description:
The Agentic RAG system in AG2 uses a multi-agent group chat: a Planner agent decomposes user queries into sub-queries, Retrieval agents fetch from vector stores (e.g., FAISS) and web APIs, a Synthesizer agent merges results with citations, and a Verifier ensures consistency. Agents communicate via AG2's enhanced group chat with speaker selection and termination conditions. This autonomous workflow handles complex, multi-hop retrieval without human intervention.

Key components include the QueryPlanner for intent decomposition, parallel Retrievers for multi-source access, and the AttributionSynthesizer for generating cited responses. Vex hooks intercept agent messages to verify retrieval faithfulness and synthesis accuracy before final output.

The architecture scales to domain-specific RAG by adding specialized retrievers (e.g., arXiv, enterprise DBs) while maintaining conversation guardrails.
architectureOverview.diagram:
User Query
    |
[GroupChat: AG2]
    |-------------------|
[Planner]  [Retrievers]  [Synthesizer]
 |          |     |        |
Sub-Queries  FAISS  WebAPI   Citations
    |-------------------|
           [Vex Hooks]
    |-------------------|
         Final Response

risks.name: Hallucination in Synthesis
risks.description: Synthesizer agent fabricates details not grounded in retrieved sources
risks.mitigation: Vex source faithfulness checks on final synthesis messages

risks.name: Query Planning Drift
risks.description: Planner generates irrelevant sub-queries leading to wrong retrievals
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic similarity verification against original user intent

risks.name: Cross-Source Inconsistency
risks.description: Conflicting facts from multiple retrievers cause erroneous consensus
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-source consistency scoring in group chat

risks.name: Citation Fabrication
risks.description: Fake or incorrect source attribution undermines trust
risks.mitigation: Vex citation verification against actual retrieval payloads

implementation.title: AG2 GroupChat Setup for Agentic RAG
implementation.description: Initialize multi-agent group with planner, retrievers, and synthesizer
implementation.code:
from ag2 import GroupChat, GroupChatManager
from ag2.agents import AssistantAgent

planner = AssistantAgent(name="Planner", llm_config={"model": "gpt-4o"})
retriever1 = AssistantAgent(name="FAISSRetriever", llm_config={"model": "gpt-4o"})
retriever2 = AssistantAgent(name="WebRetriever", llm_config={"model": "gpt-4o"})
synthesizer = AssistantAgent(name="Synthesizer", llm_config={"model": "gpt-4o"})

agents = [planner, retriever1, retriever2, synthesizer]
groupchat = GroupChat(agents=agents, messages=[], max_round=12)
manager = GroupChatManager(groupchat=groupchat, llm_config={"model": "gpt-4o"})

implementation.title: Retrieval Agent Implementation
implementation.description: FAISS retriever with source tracking for attribution

implementation.title: Synthesizer with Citation Tracking
implementation.description: Generate response with verifiable source attribution

implementation.title: Group Chat Initiation
implementation.description: Start Agentic RAG workflow with user query
implementation.code:
user_proxy = UserProxyAgent(name="User")

query = "What are the latest advancements in quantum computing error correction?"

user_proxy.initiate_chat(
    manager,
    message=query,
    clear_history=True
)
